Lexile 1000–1300L
DTR | Listen to a Speaker Support an Opinion
LSO-MS7-L1-10001300-MSSCI7-STP-AAD-02 | MS Science: Grade 7 | Fossil Record and Patterns of Life's History | Shark Tank Pitch | African & African Diaspora Voices
Pages of Stone
Click 🎧 to listen.
Listen to a conversation between two students. The girl is discussing her opinion about her fossil exhibit. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer four questions about the conversation and the evidence the girl gives to support her opinion. As you listen, you may take notes.
Listen to the Conversation
1 QUESTION 1:
Why does the girl describe the wall of rock at the museum?
2 QUESTION 2:
Where did the girl first learn that the deepest layers are the oldest?
3 QUESTION 3:
According to the girl, why are the deepest layers the oldest?
4 QUESTION 4:
What is the girl's main point about the fossil record?
Answer all 4 questions to submit.
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Identify why the girl brings up the museum. Identify where she learned about the layers. Identify what she says about why the deepest layers are the oldest. Identify her main point about the fossil record.
CA ELD: PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.7, PI.B.8, PII.A.1 | NGSS: MS-LS4-1 | MS Science: Grade 7
